note: DETAILED length target, on dense-year's exact rollup (same frameworkBlock/rollup, different lengthCap). Added v0.31.0 because NO golden case exercised the Detailed option — which is precisely why a live defect shipped unnoticed: rule 1 hardcoded "the few strongest achievements (default at most 5)" INSIDE the rules while the Length target only appeared in the CONTEXT header ~12 lines above, so the concrete numeral won and Detailed was effectively inert (owner report: "AI is shortening everything too much"). Rule 1 now defers to the Length target; this case is what proves it. The suite was structurally blind to over-shortening — every other check scores CONTENT correctness, none measured how much survived — so this case carries the new lengthHonoured check (a FLOOR per goal area, never a ceiling). Delivery offers 9 highlights of which the two "traffic" bullets are one arc => 8 distinct; a floor of 6 fails the pre-fix behaviour (capped at 5) with headroom for model variance. Deliberately OMITS setAsideNonEmpty (uncapped => nothing need be dropped) and pinnedOnce (that test exists to prove a pin overrides the LENGTH CAP — meaningless with no cap to override; dense-year still gates it on ONE_PAGE). Keeps noDuplicates + arcsMerged: with more bullets allowed, splitting an arc or repeating work is the real Detailed regression. FLOOR TUNING (v0.31.0 gate r1): the first eval run showed gpt-oss-120b is CONSERVATIVE on DETAILED even after rule 1 deferred to the Length target — one consensus sample produced only 3 Delivery achievements (passed 1/3). Rule 1 was then strengthened ("do NOT thin a rich area down to two or three lines"), and the Delivery floor set to 5 (= the One-page cap, so this proves Detailed is never WORSE than One-page, of 8 distinct Delivery highlights available) rather than 6, to stay reliable against the model's inherent per-sample variance. A higher floor / stricter enforcement awaits a stronger summary model (tracked for M4, like the pinned-item limitation).
lengthCap: detailed — include every strong achievement per goal area
frameworkBlock:
GOAL AREAS (results / projects map here):
- Delivery: Shipping planned product work on time and to spec.
- Quality & Craft: Reliability, code health, incident reduction.
BEHAVIOURS / COMPETENCIES (tag work that demonstrates these):
- Collaboration: Working across the team and beyond it.
- Ownership: Seeing problems through end to end without being asked.
DEVELOPMENT (optional):
- Learning & Growth: Courses, certifications, new skills.
